titleStmt.title: Letter from John McCann to Thomas F. Delaney, 11 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John McCann

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from John McCann to Tomas Delaney who is recovering in Portobello Hospital following Delaney's operation. McCann also sent, by separate cover, flowers, to brighten Delaney's day. Thomas Delaney worked for the Grand Canal Company of which John McCann was the governor of the court of directors.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
January 11th Simmonscourt Castle Donnybrook Dear Fr Delaney I am delighted to hear
from Fr Finn that the operation had
been a complete success. He found however
that it was quite necessary to have it
done & says that you will feel perfectly
well when you are over the effects of the
operation. I am sending you a few flowers to brighten
your room. Yours truly John McCann
Mr John McCann, Simmonscourt Castle, Donnybrook
T. Delaney Esq Portobello Hospital Dublin
